---
title: "Cryptocurrency Volatility Forecasting"
author: "Elliot Yang"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Introduction

This project develops an automated cryptocurrency volatility forecasting framework using GARCH-family models.

The workflow consists of:

1.  Downloading daily cryptocurrency prices
2.  Calculating log returns
3.  Testing stationarity using the Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) test
4.  Testing ARCH effects
5.  Estimating GARCH(1,1), GJR-GARCH(1,1), and EGARCH(1,1)
6.  Selecting the best-performing model using AIC, BIC and Log-Likelihood
7.  Forecasting future volatility
8.  Evaluating forecasting performance using RMSE and MAE
9.  Estimating 95% and 99% Value-at-Risk

## Data overview

### Cryptocurrency Returns

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
returns <- read_csv("../data/processed/cleaned_returns.csv")

glimpse(returns)
```

```{r}
head(returns)
```

## Best model

### Selected Models

```{r}
best_models <- readRDS("../outputs/models/best_models.rds")

data.frame(
  Cryptocurrency = names(best_models),
  Model = sapply(best_models,
                 function(x) x@model$modeldesc$vmodel)
)
```

## Forcast accuracy

```{r}
accuracy <- read_csv("../outputs/tables/forecast_accuracy.csv")

knitr::kable(accuracy)
```

## VaR

```{r}
var <- read_csv("../outputs/tables/var_results.csv")

knitr::kable(var)
```

## Forecast plots

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(rugarch)

# Load data
returns <- read_csv(
  "../data/processed/cleaned_returns.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

best_models <- readRDS("../outputs/models/best_models.rds")

coins <- c("BTC", "ETH", "SOL", "BNB")

split <- floor(0.8 * nrow(returns))

for (coin in coins) {

  # Load fitted model
  fit <- best_models[[coin]]

  # Load forecast results
  forecast_df <- read_csv(
    paste0("../outputs/forecasts/", coin, "_forecast.csv"),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )

  # Historical fitted volatility
 hist_sigma <- as.numeric(sigma(fit))

hist_vol <- tibble(
  Date = tail(returns$Date, length(hist_sigma)),
  Volatility = hist_sigma,
  Type = "Historical"
)

  # Forecast volatility
  forecast_vol <- tibble(
    Date = as.Date(forecast_df$Date),
    Volatility = forecast_df$Forecast_Volatility,
    Type = "Forecast"
  )

  # Only display the most recent history
  plot_df <- bind_rows(
    tail(hist_vol, 250),
    forecast_vol
  )

  forecast_start <- max(hist_vol$Date)

  p <- ggplot(plot_df,
              aes(x = Date,
                  y = Volatility,
                  colour = Type)) +

    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +

    geom_vline(
      xintercept = forecast_start,
      linetype = "dashed",
      colour = "grey50"
    ) +

    scale_colour_manual(
      values = c(
        Historical = "#1F4E79",
        Forecast = "#D62728"
      )
    ) +

    labs(
      title = paste(coin, "Conditional Volatility"),
      subtitle = "Historical fitted volatility and forecasted conditional volatility",
      x = "Date",
      y = "Conditional Volatility",
      colour = ""
    ) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

    theme(
      legend.position = "top",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)

}



```
